data.title: Top Stocks Leveraging Artificial Intelligence In March 2025
data.description:
Join Alpha Picks Today!

This video's transcript was generated by a third party. It is not curated or reviewed and is provided for convenience and information purposes only. The accuracy and completeness of the transcript are not guaranteed.

Steven Cress: Hello and welcome to Seeking Alpha. I am Steve Cress, Head of Quantitative Strategy at Seeking Alpha and I am joined today by Francesca Friday, who is a quantitative strategy writer for us. Today our focus is on artificial intelligence and our top stocks for March 2025. As you're well aware, normally we do our top stocks in January, and we did do that for our AI stocks. But being that there's been such an incredible pullback, we thought it would be really timely to highlight some of the names that are at the top of our screen now.

Before we get into it though, I just want to do a little housekeeping and go over our disclaimer. We're not advising you personally concerning the nature, potential, value or suitability of any particular security. You alone are solely responsible for determining whether any investment, security, or strategy, or any product or service is appropriate or suitable for you based on your investment objectives and personal and financial situation. This presentation is for information purposes only. Content is presented as of the date published or indicated, and may be superseded by subsequent events. It represents my opinions, which may not reflect the views of Seeking Alpha as a whole. Past performance is no guarantee of future results. Seeking Alpha is not a licensed securities dealer, broker, or US adviser or investment bank.

Thank you for your patience during that disclaimer.

For those who are new to Seeking Alpha, as I mentioned, my name is Steve Cress. I'm Head of Quantitative Strategy at Seeking Alpha. I have about 35 years of experience in the financial world. Much of that was during my tenure at Morgan Stanley, where I worked for about 13 years running a prop desk in quantitative strategies. I also founded a hedge fund and a fintech company that for lack of a better description was a robo analyst. And that was purchased by Seeking Alpha about 6 years ago. And we are also joined, as I mentioned, by Francesca Friday. Francesca, please tell everybody a little bit about yourself today.

Francesca Friday: Thanks for having me, Steve. I recently joined the quantitative strategy team. And before that, I was a senior private equity reporter at Mergermarket. And before that, I spent about 7 years at The Financial Times covering a bunch of different stuff from the markets to mergers and acquisitions and different types of alternative investments, including private equity, private credit and hedge funds.

SC: Francesca, thank you so much. And Francesca really did a great job helping us out with uncovering some of the stocks. And I'm going to tell you a little bit about how we found those stocks, why Quant works. Of course, the March market recap on the tariff fears and what's been spurring the market sell-off, the AI state of play, and our top AI stocks for March 2025. So, first we're just going to dig into Quant and how we've been able to achieve such great results for such a long period.

Quant is very similar to what fundamental analysts do. Analysts that work at Morgan Stanley, Merrill Lynch, Goldman Sachs, these are fundamental analysts that look at core values when assessing a stock. It's very similar for Quant. However, we do add a dedicated strategy and of course the power of computer processing. At Seeking Alpha, our Quant strategy is GARP, which stands for growth at a reasonable price. We also add momentum to that and positive analyst revisions. So those are the factors that we focus on, much in a similar fashion that many professional analysts do.

So we're looking for stocks within that GARP approach that are collectively strong on value, growth, profitability, and as I mentioned, EPS revisions and momentum as well. And with the powerful computer processing that we've set up at Seeking Alpha, it gives us the ability to scan all these companies in the United States and plus ADRs throughout the world. So we have a very, very large universe of stocks. And due to our powerful computer processing, we have the ability to refresh our opinions on those stocks every single day. We go through the cashflow statements, income statements, balance sheets, and hundreds of financial metrics, and we score each one of those. And when we do it, we look at a metric for a company, and we score relative to the rest of its sector. So that helps us determine the strength or weakness of a company, and that is through the powerful computer processing that we have at Seeking Alpha. So our vision is to help eliminate emotional investing and uncover top stocks using data-driven tactics.

So we are definitely in a period of heightened emotional investing. Many investors have sort of forgotten looking at fundamentals and the markets are moving off of sentiment and fear. And that's one of the reasons why we've seen such a large sell-off in the last 4 weeks. So many of the AI stocks that were the darling stocks in 2023 and 2024 have really come off sharply. And it's not because their fundamentals have changed. It's because investor sentiment has become more fearful of the environment. And as a result, individuals are seeking out cash, consumer staple stocks, utility stocks, the defensive plays that people typically in a very much of a herd mentality run to during periods like we're in now. So they sell stocks where they make profits and many people made profits in AI stocks. So a number of them have been quite weak over the last several months.

So how do we pick our AI stocks? Well, we do not have a team of professional AI experts on the quantitative strategy team, so we leave it to the ETF experts to select our universe of stocks. And what we do is we take all the stocks that are in several ETFs. We have the Global X Robotics and Artificial Intelligence ETF, ticker symbol BOTZ. We have the Robo Global Robotics and Automation Index, ticker symbol ROBO. And we also take the Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF, AIQ.

So we take all the stocks that are in those ETFs, and what we do is we run it through our powerful computer processing and our Quant model. And that's how we determine from the universe of AI stocks that are within those ETF universes, where you have the ability to compare them to each other. And we could look based on our model at who we think are strong companies and weak companies. It really comes down, as I said, to those core factors that we look at of value, growth, profitability, EPS revisions, and momentum. And the AI stocks that are strongest collectively on those factors get our highest ranking.

So we've selected 5 today. And SA has a pretty good track record using this strategy. I'm going to show you our performance over time. If you look back to 2010, to date, you can see that if you invested $10,000 in our Strong Buy stocks back in 2010, that $10,000 would be worth $300,000 today. Conversely, if you invested it in the S&P, that same $10,000 would only be worth about $58,000. So our Quant system has worked very well. And it does work well across sectors. So we're very pleased with the track record that we have specifically. We've read a number of articles on top AI stocks going back to early 2024, and they tend to do very, very well. So that's why I'm pleased to bring to you our top 5 in March.

So a little bit more about what's happened with the market. As I mentioned, it's really been trading off of fear and sentiment. And markets have really been reacting to tariffs for the most part and indirectly inflation that could be a result of tariffs. And earlier, a couple - say it was in early January, we had information coming out from DeepSeek, which really looked like it was going to change the playing field for AI, in that their infrastructure was a lot less demanding in terms of power through its search mechanism.

And as a result of that – put up a scare out in many AI stocks where billions and billion dollars have been invested and many energy supply companies have done very well too, because data center demand for energy has been so strong. So it really rocked not only the AI stocks, it rocked a broader set of stocks as well. So, a selloff in the tech sector and President Donald Trump's rapid shift in tariff policies have really rattled the market. And it's been bogged down by this lingering inflation, which we've been worried about for the last several years. It was coming down, but it seems to have halt that at these levels. And of course, many economists and strategists know that tariffs tend to introduce inflation back into the market. So the markets are very nervous about that.

US inflation did inch up to 3% in January from 2.9% in December and the tariffs hadn't even been enacted at that point. Topping market forecasts, which were around 2.9%. The Fed has indicated that interest rates currently in a range of 4.25% to 4.5% are likely to hold steady in March. Economists were looking for three interest rate cuts in 2025. It looked like during January, February, and going into March that may be moved off the table, there might be one cut. Of course, with the introduction of tariffs, there's really no way to predict what's going to happen at this point. Individuals and investors are worried about anywhere from looking at inflation issues and interest rate issues, but perhaps seeing a slowdown in the economy, which could result –in a recession. So really, the market is reacting to a number of different factors, there's a lot of uncertainty.

U.S. tech stocks have begun to recover some of the losses from the DeepSeek market shakeup, but the volatility has continued. And we've seen some companies come in with really good earnings, including heavy weights like NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Super Micro, which are all very AI-centric and the stocks have slid tremendously.

FF: Steve, I just had a quick question for you.

SC: Yeah. Sure.

FF: We're seeing the stocks of some of those AI heavyweights like NVIDIA fall, a lot of times those big companies are listed as a hold in our Quant system. Do you see NVIDIA, for example, is more attractive now that the valuation is coming down?

SC: Well, the valuation we could say is certainly a lot more attractive than where it was. And actually, I'll take us to our Seeking Alpha platform. And we're going to put in NVIDIA in here, so people can see exactly where it is on a real-time basis. So the stock is up today about 1.65%. And if I click on year-to-date, we can see the stock is off 16% year-to-date. So it has come off a lot. The S&P 500 is probably down about 1% year-to-date, maybe a little bit more. So you can see NVIDIA being down 16% has really been shaken. And their numbers were pretty good. The problem that we have with the Seeking Alpha Quant is you can see on the right hand side, there's a Hold on it. Wall Street analysts have a Strong Buy. Our Seeking Alpha contributors consensus is a Buy in the stock.

Now, if we look at our factor grades, we could see an F for value. And when we look at our factor grades, I just want to let people know this is actually all sector relative. So we're looking at this relative to the sector. So the value of Nvidia is very expensive. That's what that F represents compared to the sector. However, the growth is very strong versus the sector. Profitability with an A+ is incredibly strong versus the sector. The momentum of the stock is actually in line with the sector, but analysts have had positive revisions as we can see from that A-. So the revisions and the rate that they're being moved up is superior to the sector, hence that A- grade. So I want to take us into valuation.

And if we were to look at this couple months ago, Francesca, this actually would have been almost all red with the exception of the PEG ratios. So right now, we do see P/E on a non-GAAP basis for trailing is a D+. It's 36x versus the sector 22x. If we look at that in that top row, we can see it's currently 36x. If we look to the far right, we actually also show you what the five-year average P/E is. And it is 63x. So it's actually at a 41% discount to its five-year average. So it's really, compared to its five-year average, it is a lot more attractive. However, compared to the sector, as you can see at 36x, and with the sector at 22x, the P/E is at a 62% premium to the sector, which makes it pretty unattractive.

However, if we start looking at P/E on a non-GAAP basis and P/E on a GAAP basis, it's really starting to become in line with the sector. And really for years, like I have not seen this, this has been really Fs and D-s across the board. So it is becoming a lot more attractive than it was. And based on the five-year average, which you see on the far right side, you could see it's trading at a discount to those levels, where it was much more expensive. So the answer is, it is certainly more attractive than where it was. However, when we're looking at the Quant grades overall, it really is weighed down when we look at it compared to the sector. So it's still incredibly expensive. If you do own the stock though, I will remind people, one of my favorite valuation metrics is PEG, and it is an A on a PEG basis. PEG combines 2 metrics together, where we look at P/E and the growth of the company.

So it's a great way of looking at the valuation framework plus the growth framework and combining it together. And on that basis, it's actually pretty attractive. So I'd certainly say stick to the Hold. But if you're a big believer in PEG, that would indicate that the value is actually pretty attractive. I'm going to take us back to our presentation.

And what I’m going to pick up is, where I had mentioned before that, really since the end of January, and really more so over the last couple of weeks, over the last month, there's been a big rotation within the investment community into the defensive stocks. And you can see the defensive stocks, which are typically consumer staples, utilities, healthcare, real estate, those are the few sectors that are in the positive domain. Consumer staples in the last 4 weeks up 4.21%, real estate up 3.8%. Conversely, if you look at the bottom, technology in the last 4 weeks is down 5.3%. Consumer discretionary is down 7.75%. These are huge moves for entire sectors. I mean, you rarely see this kind of move in entire sectors over a 4 week period. So it really shows the extent of how nervous the market is. And again, the market is not paying attention to fundamentals. It's making its moves based on fear and sentiment, hence the rotation to these defensive sectors.

So we have had explosive growth in AI and I don't want people to forget that. And that really is not going to change anytime soon. Even with the tariffs or inflation fears or interest rate fears, there is tremendous growth. So Francesca, I want to let you share a little bit about what you've picked up in the sector. So I'll leave it with you.

FF: Sure. So our colleagues on the Quant team put together this handy chart where you can see just how far AI is expected to reach by 2030, $538 billion to $1.8 trillion to be exact. Currently North America has the largest market share with 37%, but a big hotbed of growth we're seeing is Asia Pacific, which is projected to grow at a CAGR of 19.8% through 2034. So very impressive growth.

Now, just to go over a little bit of the investment trends we're seeing as of March 2025. Now, Steve touched earlier on the emergence of DeepSeek. It's a low cost AI chatbot, and that's really challenged Silicon Valley's dominance in the space, given that DeepSeek came onto the scene being able to do what say Google Gemini or Microsoft's OpenAI has done for way cheaper and way faster, to put it simply. So that has sent firms scrambling to develop their own cheap models through a process called distillation. That's basically a fancy word for machine learning. And it's when you take a more complex advanced teacher model, and that transfers knowledge to a smaller, more efficient student model so they can do this on a more efficient scale.

Now 2025 is also predicted to be a year of agentic AI compared to generative AI, which was big between 2023 and 2024. And agentic AI involves autonomous AI agents, which are capable of competing complex tasks which impact workflows across industries. So that's something you're going to see coming up a lot with some of these AI startups and big competitors in the space.

So DeepSeek poses a major threat to US AI developers. But one thing which you'll see reflected in our top 5 picks for March is that if you are an AI company trying to compete in 2025 and you can find maybe not at the front, but a valuable place in the overall value chain, that is a recipe for success. So when I say that, I mean hardware makers, like semiconductor design and manufacturers, or companies getting involved in hyperscalers with cloud services, data centers, and large language models. So there's a lot of ways that you can still be successful with AI in this market. That doesn't mean necessarily leading the pack and competing directly with DeepSeek.

So not all AI stocks are created equal. If you look back at 2024, the same AI stocks that crushed the market are now dealing with a lot of market volatility and valuation concerns. Steve went deep into Nvidia, which ended the year in triple digits and now is down nearly 18%. So that's the perfect example right there. And if you look at the Mag-7, which ended 2024 up 57%, is down around 10. 5% so far this year. So if you look at the ETFs that we mentioned earlier, they're exhibiting a lot of mixed performance. For example, the Defiance Quantum ETF, QTUM, and Invesco Semiconductors ETF, PSI, have outperformed the market since 2020. While if you look at iShares future AI and tech ETF, which is another big one that has underperformed.

SC: Yeah. And I think really the sector is just underperforming in general. So these ETFs are separate than the ones that we chose the AI stocks from, but even those AI centric ETFs have come off quite a bit. So, all the way around, it has not been good for technology. Back to you.

FF: Right. If you look at AI ETFs specifically, they're underperforming the broader market on a year-to-date basis. So what's all this to say, basically, selecting stocks with strong fundamentals that are either already beating the market or well positioned for strong returns once conditions stabilize is more important than ever. And as you'll see, and as Steve has touched on, that's where Seeking Alpha's quant system can come in and be helpful in that selection process.

So for our…

SC: So here we go, yeah, I'm going to set it up for you, Francesca. This is, again, we picked these stocks based on those ETFs that we had mentioned, and we put it through our algo. And our algo compares each of these companies to other companies in the technology sector. And that's how we came up with this list. So I'm going to hand it back to you, Francesca.

FF: Right. So if you put all 5 of these stocks together so far over the past 12 months, they've returned an average of 47.4%. And on a year-to-date basis, they've returned 74.6%, which is impressive when you consider that over the past 12 months, the S&P has only returned 12.9%. And as of year-to-date, given all the market disruptions we've discussed, it's down negative 2.3.

So our first pick is called Celestica. We have a market cap of $10.5 billion. It's a Strong Buy. You'll see that all of the stocks in this presentation are Strong Buys based on the Quant rankings. It's in the information technology sector, ranked number 12 out of 559. And it's in the electronic manufacturing services industry, and it is leading 1 out of 16 there. So what is Celestica? It's a leader in supply chain solutions. It's one of the largest electronic manufacturing services companies and had one of Canada's biggest tech IPOs. So it's up 73% over the past year, which is impressive when you look at the current market. And it's capitalizing on the AI boom as a top performing Alpha Pick. So it's up 200% since we first added this to the Alpha Pick portfolio in October, 2023, which is a great growth trajectory.

Steve, earlier in this presentation talked about the importance of PEG and why we really like that metric here on the team. So it has a PEG of 0.64, which is a negative 61.65% difference from the sector median. And as he explained, a PEG underneath 1 typically insinuates that it is undervalued compared to its peers. So it is a good deal here. And another highlight I wanted to point out is that it has six-month price performance of 98%, which is a 2,400% difference. So the performance we're seeing here in the momentum is really impressive compared to what else is on the market right now.

SC: Yeah. Francesca, I think I'll go back into our platform here to sort of give a demonstration of where it is. So we're looking at Celestica and maybe the market's listening to us today, the stock is up 2.72%. But I have a feeling with the S&P coming back up a little bit and individuals trying to figure out what's going on with the tariffs and thinking – mentioning the tariffs being that this stock is based in Canada, it is one of the reasons why it did come off so sharply. But it is a global company. So especially with global tech companies it’s really hard to pin tariffs on many of them unless they're like dedicated to in semiconductors where there have been specific tariffs. So you can see it has really traded off with the sentiment. And the last month, the stock is down 39%. So getting completely walloped off of that sentiment.

However, as you can see, if you look at the ratings summary, the consensus from Seeking Alpha Analyst and Wall Street is a Buy in stock. And of course, the Quant is a Strong Buy. If you would look at the stock at a one-year, let's look at it at a six-month basis. The stock on a six-month basis is up 96%. And if we go back to the factor grade card on the right side, we could see that the valuation framework is the exact same now as it was 6 months ago. So the fact that the stock has gone up 96% really should not play into the investment decision. You want to look at what the current valuation framework is and you want to look at the growth framework. So, and that's even more important. If we look at the growth, you could see now the grade is A-. And if you look 6 months ago, it was C+. So the growth is actually far superior now for Celestica than it was just 6 months ago, yet the valuation framework is exactly the same. And if I click on growth, you are going to see a great report card.

So whether you're looking at revenue growth on a year-over-year basis or forward basis, it's very strong compared to the sector. Revenue growth on a forward basis is 16% versus the sector at 6.5%. And then if we go to the bottom line and we look at EPS GAAP on a forward basis, it gets an A grade because the forward GAAP growth is 38% compared to the sector at 10%. And with the growth estimates, we're looking at analyst consensus estimates for growth, whether it's revenue, or EPS, or EBITDA. We update that on a daily basis. So analysts firmly believe that this company is going to be stronger than the rest of the sector. So it looks really good on that basis. I'm going to take us back to the slide deck and then we'll go to the next slide, Francesca.

FF: Great explanation, Steve. So up next, our second pick, and these are in no particular order, by the way, they're not ranked by level of importance, is FARO Technologies. It has a market cap of $589 million, so more mid cap than our previous pick. Also a Strong Buy. It's also in information technology, ranked number 17 out of 545. It's also electronic equipment and instruments, 1 out of 47. And what does this do exactly? It's leveraging AI to be a leader in smart factories and intelligent automation. So this is what we were talking about earlier. These companies are using AI in new, innovative and different ways that you wouldn't necessarily expect. So they have AI scanning technology, which helps increase productivity. And this company has rallied over 80% in the last 6 months. So a lot of great momentum there. And as you can see, it has a B+ in growth. And one great stat is that it has a forward EBIT growth of 208%, which is a 1,839% difference from that sector median. So a lot of great growth happening there.

SC: Awesome. And I’ll take us to our next one. Go ahead.

FF: Next, we have Netflix. I don't have to explain to you what Netflix is. Market cap of $424 billion. It's in the communication sector, 3 out of 231. And it's ranked first in movies and entertainment right now out of 38 total companies. So Netflix's Q4 earnings were excellent. They added 19 million new paid memberships and raked in 44% year-over-year growth. And that was doubling its 9.2 million expected gains. So really great results there. You might wonder why we're considering Netflix as an AI company. Well, it uses AI and machine learning to grow and retain its subscriber base by offering viewers personalized recommendations. So when you open up Netflix, that is not the same home screen everybody's seeing. They are serving you very, very specific recommendations based on this AI algorithm that is constantly being updated to give you the best preferences and keep you paying them.

And if you look at EPS revisions, you'll see they have an A grade there, and they have 35 positive EPS revisions and 5 down revisions from analysts over the past 90 days, which means we're not the only ones that are confident in Netflix's upward trajectory. And they have $7.36 billion in cash from operations and a forward EBITDA growth of 28.3%, which is a 917.5% difference. So great profitability, great growth, great momentum, and great revisions. And I'll let Steve take you further into the details there.

SC: Yeah, I'm going to jump onto the platform. And before I go there, I'll just show people. If you look at the Quant rating history card, Netflix is a stock that we rarely get to see as a Quant Buy or a Strong Buy because the valuation is always so expensive. And as I mentioned, we look for stocks that are collectively strong on value, growth and profitability, but many of the Magnificent 7 stocks tend to be just too darn expensive for our model. So our model has done really well, as you saw from its track record, but we usually have a difficult time getting the Mag 7 stocks in there.

So this is a period, the few times that we can really say that the model will jump into the Mag 7 stocks is typically whenever we have a market pullback or a correction. And in this case, we've definitely have experienced this with Netflix. As you can see on the chart here, it's pulled back. And if we look at the factor grades on the right hand side, you can see that the valuation grade is a D now. 6 months ago, it was an F. And one of the things that's great about this is that the valuation framework is now better than it was 6 months ago. But you can also see for growth the grade is A- compared to being a B+ 6 months ago. So the growth is actually trended up while the valuation has improved, hence giving us the ability to have that Strong Buy recommendation.

I’m going to pop into the platform here. We're going to go to Netflix on Seeking Alpha’s platform. So we could see in the last month, the stock is down about 11.89%. And obviously, just on the chart, you could see the big drop after March 5th. So when I take a look at the company, and we want to look at the underlying metrics. And by the way, one of the things that you can do if you're a Seeking Alpha Premium customer, you could look at exactly where the company is in its sector. So this is in the communication service sector, and it's within the movies and entertainment industry. Within the industry, it ranks 1 out of 38. So you can actually click on that and see all the other companies that we have ranked by our Quant system and exactly where they rank in our model for that particular industry or for that sector.

So if we go back for Netflix, we're going to dig into the valuation here. It's only 1 click away. And we're going to take a look at P/E on a non-GAAP basis. So it's still a D-, and we could see that's at 36x. So it is expensive versus the sector. But as I mentioned, it looks better now than it did 6 months ago. And it also looks better than where it was on its 5 year average. So currently it's at 36x. And if you go to the far right, you can see the Netflix average P/E over the last 5 years is 44x. So this is actually at a 17% discount. So it's a historical average. So it’s been improving versus the sector in the last 6 months and definitely more attractive than where it was 5 years ago.

Of course, when we look at the PEG, we're not seeing what we are in some of the other IT companies in terms of As or Bs, but it is in line with the sector on a PEG basis. So not all of these metrics are equal weighted. We do place a greater weight on metrics that have a higher predictive value. So being that PEG is one of our favorite ratios, it’s a spoiler alert, that does have a little bit of a higher weight. And hence that helps bring up the overall grade to D. If it were to be a D-, it would actually default back to hold. So this is probably going to be just very brief period in our Quant system that we see Netflix as a strong buy. And that's because the market has pulled back.

Back to our slide deck and stock number 4. Francesca?

FF: Thanks, Steve. So we have another Mag-7. I don't have to explain to you what this is. Amazon is a $2.2 trillion company and currently ranked 7 out of 481 in the consumer discretionary sector. And it's in the broadline retail industry, number 2 out of 27. Just like Netflix, super strong Q4 results with $1.86 billion (sic) in EPS, beating expectations by 0.38, excuse me, not $1.86 billion. And the one thing that's most interesting to us from an AI perspective about Amazon right now is its AWS cloud unit, which has demonstrated excellent performance with 48% year-over-year operating income growth and $39.8 billion in trailing operating income. And so the AWS data centers will profit from AI advancements post DeepSeek. The further along AI technology goes, the more these hyperscaler startups and bigger companies like Amazon will profit. And looking at EPS revisions, a lot of analysts are also in agreement with our Quant system. It has 35 up EPS revisions and 16 down in the last 90 days.

SC: And if we look at the factor card on the right side, the factor grades, you can see the current valuation grade is D versus 6 months ago, that was an F, and 3 months ago, it was a D-. So whenever we have a valuation D- or lower, the stock automatically is a Hold because it's just too expensive. However, the valuation framework has improved slightly and just slightly enough to put it into a Strong Buy territory. But I will also add the growth grade, which is A-, is stronger than where it was 6 months ago at B+. So a company this size, you might not think it's a big move going from B+ to A-, but it is pretty sizable. So when you look at together on the improvement on a growth basis and the valuation framework, it puts it in the Strong Buy territory.

And Francesca, stock number 5.

FF: All right. Our last stock is called Okta, and it has a market cap of $20 billion. It is also ranked a strong buy. In the technology sector, it's 14 out of 545 companies. And it's in the internet services and infrastructure industry, and it's ranked 4 out of 22. So unlike Netflix and Amazon, this one might not be a household name necessarily, but it's a software company offering businesses the tools to secure their employees' identities through machine learning and AI, which is a really important area these days. Everyone's talking about cyber security. This is a leading company that combines cyber security with AI. And we've seen it rally 16% over the past month, and 41% year-to-date while some of its rivals have struggled. It became profitable last year as part of a big turnaround strategy that involved better integrating AI into its identity management systems. They made a lot of cost cuts. They did a lot to increase their margins in bottom line and we've seen that reflected in the numbers. So they have an impressive 39 positive EPS revisions over the past 90 days with only one negative. You can see analysts are also very aligned with our Strong Buy assessment. And if you look from a profitability standpoint, the levered FCF margin is 33.28%, a 180% difference from the sector median in terms of that free cash flow.

SC: All right. And last but not least, stock number 6. That's it. Only 5 stocks. Not 6. So we did all top 5 there. And I guess we're going to go into Q&A. We had a couple of questions. And Francesca, if you put the questions forward, I will answer them for you.

FF: Sure. So we had someone named MattButlerUSA write in. He's new to Quant, and he was curious when we put out a list. This is both for presentations, but also for our written articles. Is there a specific ranking when you number these 1 through 5?

SC: So when we number them 1 through 5, we're putting the 5 stocks forward that we're highlighting. The strength is actually in the Quant ranking itself. So when you're on the Seeking Alpha Premium platform, you'll actually see what number is there. And that will indicate to you of those strong buys, which one has the highest weight.

FF: That makes sense. So we had 2 similar questions come in, one from KareemHashem and one from Go4Launch, and both of them are bullish on Credo. CRDO is the ticker, which pops up a lot in our analysis, So they're curious where it went and if you're still interested in this stock.

SC: All right. So let's take us to the Seeking Alpha platform and we're going to put in CRDO. Everyone will be able to see exactly where we are on it. So as you can see on the stock page, if you look on the far right side, the Quant still has a Strong Buy on it. And yes, the stock has come off quite a bit. It's trading sort of in sympathy, but more so than many of the other IT stocks for various reasons. But the company continues to deliver as I scroll down and you'll see they just reported on March 4th. Credo's non-GAAP EPS came in at $0.25 and it beat by - analysts estimates by $0.07 and revenue beat by -- came in at $14.6 million beating by $135 million -- I'm sorry, out of 14.64 billion, that should probably be.

So, we're going to actually find out exactly what it is. We're going to look at the revenues right here. So we can see where the company is. So that's $193 million. So there was a little bit of a typo on our report there. So that's what's great about Seeking Alpha. We have a wonderful platform. You can look at the income statement, balance sheet, cashflow within one click. And of course, if you look at the growth card, you can see it is an A+ report card on growth. And this is one of the reasons why we like Credo so much. If you look at the forward revenue growth rate, it's an A+ grade. So forward revenue growth is at 54% versus the sector at 6%. And then if we look at EPS on a forward basis, again, it's A+. The forward EPS growth rate is 183% versus the sector at 11%. So this stock is an A+ for growth.

And if we look at the valuation, the valuation compared to 6 months ago has actually improved. The current value grade is B. 6 months ago, it was a C+. So it's actually inexpensive compared to where it was 6 months ago. We'll click on that card. And you can see it's a little bit, it's not intuitive on this card. And one of the reasons why is you do see a lot of Fs and Ds and this throws individuals off. When you look at the P/E or if you look at EV/EBITDA, it does look very expensive. However, on a PEG basis, because the growth is so strong for the company, when we combine it with the P/E valuation, it gives us that A grade at 0.75. So it's at a 54% discount to the sector. So the weighting, again here is not equal weighted. There is a higher emphasis on metrics that do have a greater predictive value to it. Hence, it brings that overall grade up to B. And you can see it's more attractive now than where it was 6 months ago. So again, the growth looks fantastic here.

And if you look at the momentum of the stock over the longer term, over the last 52 weeks, it's an A+. It's up 92% versus the sector, which has been down 4.8% over that one year period. And the sector we're looking at is IT. So IT has actually not performed that well over the last -- and that's the sector median, I should point out. Over the last 9 months, it's outperformed, and over the last 6 months, it's outperformed. But in the last 3 months, the stock has gotten completely clobbered despite coming through with earnings announcements that beat expectations. So we still like the stock from a Quant perspective.

Back to our slideshow.

FF: And our last question, which you touched a little bit on earlier, Steve, people are curious about CLS, Celestica, because it is a Canadian company.

SC: Yeah.

FF: So tariffs are a concern for certain readers.

SC: Yeah, so it is a global company and the tariffs are just really hard to predict in general. We don't know if we're doing it, we don't know if we're not doing it. I feel like we've gone back and forth 4x in the last 4 weeks on the Canadian tariffs. And again, this is a global company. So it's hard to say where the impact is going to be hit on this, but certainly the stock has traded off significantly. The stock has come off a lot more than what the tariff increases are. So even if they did have full tariff increases, I don't think that the earnings and the revenue for the company would come off nearly as much as the stock did.

FF: Good to know. That makes sense.

SC: And I think that sort of brings us towards the end of the presentation. Of course, this could be a lot of work for individuals. When you're on the Seeking Alpha platform, you're doing your own research and you're in uncertain times. A lot of people may want somebody else to do the work for them. So we put together a product called Alpha Picks in July of 2022. And what we do there is each month we bring you our favorite two Quant recommendations. So on the trading day, closest to the first of the month and to the 15th of the month, we provide you what our favorite Quant Strong Buy is, and that is a separate platform. It's called Alpha Picks. And the criteria that we have are a little bit different than the other strong buys. We don't invest in REITs. We look for companies that have a market cap above $500 million. The average daily dollar volume has to be above 10 million. And we're looking basically we've set this up for long-term holders. So when a stock is recommended, we tend to keep it on the list for a while. In fact, even if the rating drops to a Hold, we'll keep it in the portfolio for a period of 180 days. Once it's at a Hold for 180 days, we'll then take it out of the portfolio and make room for other stocks. So if you're looking for us to do the work for you, I would say come to Alpha Picks.

And here is the performance on Alpha Picks. You can see since inception, it's up 138% versus the S&P, which is up 54%. So really great lead. I will mention, and I'll be very upfront, a lot of the stocks at Alpha Picks that have done very well over a long period have been coming off sharply recently. And again, that's sort of where the opportunity lies. The fundamentals for most of these companies is very much intact. For the most recent quarter, they beat both top line and bottom line expectations. And the stocks and their fundamentals look good, but because of fear and sentiment in the overall markets, people have been selling stocks where they've made money and they've been rotating it into either the defensive sectors or cash or gold. And markets always return to fundamentals. When this environment becomes normalized, and when investors start looking for companies with good fundamentals again, we anticipate many of our stocks will do extraordinarily well.

And that is really it for our presentation. I think I hit the highlights on Alpha Picks. So again, I'll just bring us back to the slide where you could look at our top 5 AI stocks for March 2025. We have OKTA, we have FARO, we have Netflix, Amazon, and Celestica. And most of them, as you saw, are trading fairly well today in the markets as the S&P is rebounding slightly.

Thank you so much for your time today. We really appreciate it. And those who are members at Seeking Alpha Premium, or at Alpha Picks, we really appreciate your membership.

FF: Thanks.

SC: Have a great week, everybody, and stay calm and carry on.

Join Alpha Picks Today!
